name: Ranked Matchups (Top Games)
description: Cross-sport interestingness curator. Pulls upcoming games per enabled sport, scores them by rank pair / favorites / spread (and Phase 3+: rivalry, narrative), matches to Dispatcharr channels via EPG, and renames+groups them into a Top Matchups channel profile so your guide shows only the games worth watching.
author: Jake (with Claude)

fields.label: Sport Sources
fields.description: Toggle which sports/leagues the curator pulls from.

fields.label: NCAA Football (CFBD)
fields.help_text: AP Top-25 + games + betting lines from CollegeFootballData.com. Offseason → no rows produced. Requires the CFBD API key below.

fields.label: NCAA Men's Basketball (CollegeBasketballData)
fields.help_text: AP Top-25 + games + betting lines from CollegeBasketballData.com. Same Bearer token as CFBD — flip both on with one key. Season runs Nov-Apr; offseason returns no rows.

fields.label: English Premier League (EPL)
fields.help_text: Football-Data.org primary, The Odds API for spread signal. Requires the Football-Data.org API key below.

fields.label: EFL Championship
fields.help_text: Second tier of English football. Standings position used as the rank slot — top 6 makes playoffs. Requires the Football-Data.org API key below.

fields.label: UEFA Champions League
fields.help_text: Knockout/group stage. Position-as-rank doesn't apply cleanly so favorites + odds carry the signal.

fields.label: Bundesliga (Germany, top flight)
fields.help_text: 18 teams, 34 matchdays. Top 4 → UCL, 5-6 → Europa/Conference, 16 → relegation playoff, 17-18 → direct relegation. Requires Football-Data.org key.

fields.label: La Liga (Spain, top flight)
fields.help_text: 20 teams, 38 matchdays. Top 4 → UCL, 5-7 → Europa/Conference, bottom 3 → relegation. Requires Football-Data.org key.

fields.label: Serie A (Italy, top flight)
fields.help_text: 20 teams, 38 matchdays. Top 4 → UCL, 5-7 → Europa/Conference, bottom 3 → relegation. Requires Football-Data.org key.

fields.label: Ligue 1 (France, top flight)
fields.help_text: 18 teams, 34 matchdays. Top 3 → UCL (4th = UCL playoff), 5 → Europa, bottom 3 → relegation. Requires Football-Data.org key.

fields.label: NHL (regular season + Stanley Cup Playoffs)
fields.help_text: Regular season uses standings points (95+ bubble, 100+ playoff-secured, 110+ division pace, 125+ Presidents' Trophy). Playoffs are best-of-7 each round. No API key required — official api-web.nhle.com.

fields.label: API Keys
fields.description: Paste your keys below (masked in the UI). Settings take precedence; if blank, the plugin falls back to <plugin_dir>/<keyname>_api_key on disk (chmod 600).

fields.label: CollegeFootballData.com API key
fields.help_text: Register: https://collegefootballdata.com/key (free, 1k req/day). The same key works for collegebasketballdata.com — one key powers both NCAAF and NCAAM, including the Monte Carlo importance signal (Phase D.2/D.3).

fields.label: Football-Data.org API key
fields.help_text: Register: https://www.football-data.org/client/register (free, 10 req/min). Free tier covers EPL, EFL Championship, UCL, Bundesliga, La Liga, Serie A, Ligue 1, and 5 more competitions.

fields.label: The Odds API key (optional)
fields.help_text: Register: https://the-odds-api.com (free 500 req/mo). Powers the close-game / spread signal across all enabled sports. If blank, that signal is skipped — other signals still fire.

fields.label: Anthropic API key (optional)
fields.help_text: Register: https://console.anthropic.com — used for two things, both optional: (1) EPG-to-channel matching when the regex pre-filter is ambiguous (cheap, Haiku, ~$0.0001 per ambiguous match), (2) LLM-rewritten EPG descriptions when 'Enable LLM-rewritten EPG descriptions' below is on. Only required if either is engaged.

fields.label: Claude model
fields.help_text: Model used for ambiguous EPG matching and (if enabled) narrative scoring.

fields.label: Curation
fields.description: How many games make the cut and which teams always show.

fields.label: Favorite teams (comma-separated)
fields.help_text: Substring-matched against home/away team names (with team-qualifier whitelist for soccer suffixes like 'FC', 'AFC', 'City', 'United'). Always shown regardless of rank, with a flat score boost. Teams in playoff races still get the 'impact-on-favorite' signal automatically when they sit near a favorite's position.

fields.label: Max curated games per refresh
fields.help_text: Top-N games by score across all enabled sports (20-50 is a sensible range; lower = tighter curation, higher = more coverage). Favorites guaranteed inclusion above this cap.

fields.label: Lookahead window (days)
fields.help_text: How far ahead to pull games. 7 = next week. Bigger = more games to score.

fields.label: Local timezone (IANA name)
fields.help_text: IANA tz name like 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Tokyo', 'Australia/Sydney'. Full list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones. Falls back to UTC if the name isn't recognized. Used to determine which games are 'today' and to format kickoff times in the EPG description.

fields.label: LLM-rewritten EPG descriptions
fields.help_text: When on, Claude rewrites each curated game's EPG description as a 2-3 sentence natural-prose preview instead of the deterministic 'Matchday X. Tagline. Source: ...' layout. Uses the Anthropic key + 'Claude model' setting above; falls back to the deterministic description if the key is missing or the API call fails. Cached per game in llm_descriptions_cache.json so a re-apply doesn't re-bill. Cost: ~$0.001 per game (Haiku 4.5), about $2-3/month at default refresh cadence.

fields.label: Interestingness Weights
fields.description: Each signal contributes to a 0-10 score. Tweak if a signal feels under/over-weighted. Score breakdown is stored per-game in cache.json so you can inspect.

fields.label: Rank-pair weight
fields.help_text: Multiplier on the rank-pair signal (both ranked > one ranked > neither).

fields.label: Close-game weight
fields.help_text: Multiplier on the close-game signal. For soccer (h2h moneyline path) this is devigged bookmaker probabilities × 2 × min(P_home, P_away), in [0, 1] where 1 = perfect coinflip. For NCAAF / NCAAM (point-spread path) the raw spread normalizes into the same [0, 1] range. Default 3.0 calibrated against the B.3 coinflip-ness formula; pre-B.3 was 0.1 against the old (14-spread)/2 formula. Keep in sync with scoring.Weights.spread.

fields.label: Favorite-team boost
fields.help_text: Flat points added when one of your favorite teams is involved. Keep in sync with scoring.Weights.favorite.

fields.label: Rivalry boost
fields.help_text: Flat points added for known rivalry games (future: rivalry DB).

fields.label: Tournament-stage weight
fields.help_text: Knockout-stage cup games (R16, QF, SF, Final). UCL etc.

fields.label: LLM narrative weight
fields.help_text: Optional LLM-judged narrative score. Disabled by default — the Monte Carlo importance signal covers structural standings stakes. Set > 0 to enable Claude-based narrative scoring for storyline / form / managerial-drama factors that the structural simulator can't capture.

fields.label: Monte Carlo importance weight
fields.help_text: Multiplier on Lahvička match-importance: |Kendall tau-c| × consequence weight, summed across both playing teams' outcome bands AND any in-league favorites' outcome bands (so a non-favorite game that swings a favorite's relegation chance gets credit structurally). Only fires for sources that implement simulation (currently soccer). Phase C.4 retired the legacy stakes / impact-on-favorite / late-season-multiplier signal family — this signal replaces all three. Set to 0 to disable.

fields.label: Importance Monte Carlo iterations
fields.help_text: Number of season simulations per match for the importance signal. 500 ≈ 0.5s per match on the test EPL season; total refresh cost stays under 30s for a typical 25-match curated list. 1000 doubles compute time but tightens tau-c convergence (Lahvička 2012 used 10000 for academic-grade stability). Drop to 200 if scheduler runs feel sluggish.

fields.label: Apply Behavior
fields.description: How matched channels are surfaced in Dispatcharr.

fields.label: Target group name
fields.help_text: Apply creates virtual channels in this ChannelGroup, cloning streams from the matched source channels (source channels untouched). A matching dummy EPGSource is created with the same name; each virtual channel gets a ProgramData entry whose description explains WHY the game made the cut. Tip: prefix with '!' (e.g. '!Top Matchups') so it sorts to the top of your group list. If you rename the group, the next apply will detect the old group/source by tvg_id markers and clean them up automatically.

fields.label: Starting channel number (0 = auto)
fields.help_text: First channel number assigned to virtual channels. Set to 0 to auto-pick (start at the highest existing non-virtual channel + 1) so the plugin slots in cleanly without colliding with your real channels. Or pin a fixed number — e.g. 9000 — if you want them at a known location. Today's games occupy the lowest numbers in this range so they appear first in any IPTV client.

fields.label: Placeholder threshold (0-10)
fields.help_text: Games scoring above this get a placeholder channel even if no EPG match exists yet — appears in your guide as 'channel match pending', so big upcoming games (like Wrexham vs Middlesbrough) show up before the provider EPG publishes the broadcast info.

fields.label: Dry run on Apply
fields.help_text: When true, 'Apply' logs intended changes but does not write to the DB.

fields.label: Auto-Refresh
fields.description: Auto-refresh keeps the curated list fresh as ranks/games/EPG update.

fields.label: Auto-refresh + apply on schedule
fields.help_text: If on, runs refresh + apply at every time listed in 'Scheduled times'.

fields.label: Scheduled times (HHMM, comma-separated)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated 4-digit times in your local timezone. e.g. '0400' = 4 AM only; '0000,0600,1200,1800' = every 6 hours. Same format as event_channel_managarr.

actions.label: Refresh curated list
actions.description: Pull games from enabled sport sources, score, EPG-match to Dispatcharr channels, write cache.json. No DB writes to channels.

actions.label: Apply to Dispatcharr
actions.description: Use cache to update target channel profile membership and (if enabled) rename matched channels. Honors dry_run.

actions.label: Refresh + apply now
actions.description: Run refresh then apply end-to-end. The daily scheduler invokes this; this button lets you trigger it on demand.

actions.label: Show current state
actions.description: Print recent cache contents with score breakdown per game. No writes, no API calls.
